WARNING: These test points are for A2918 (820-02757) ONLY. For other boards, use that board's diagnostic files. Component designators and test points are board-specific — the same rail appears at DIFFERENT components on other boards.
description: Known and anticipated fault patterns for A2918 based on M3 Pro topology, A2681 M2 Air fault data patterns (adapted), and schematic analysis. Test points use A2918-specific designators from FCT pages 113-114.
data_source: Schematic analysis + fault pattern adaptation from A2681 Safan diagnostics. No direct A2918 Safan diagnostic data found.

faults.name: CD3217 (UF400/UF500) failure

symptoms.dcps_20v: 0.001A or normal (if only one port affected)

root_causes: CD3217 internal failure (only 5V, no PD negotiation)
faults.fix: Replace CD3217 (UF400 or UF500). Ensure matching ROM.
faults.note: A2918 uses COLTRANE_CRAKE variant of CD3217 on page 71.

faults.name: PP3V8_AON short (VDDMAIN / branches)
root_causes: WiFi/BT (Willamette) module pulling PP3V8_AON_WLBT to ground
HDMI circuit (UH700 Cobra) short on PP3V8_AON_HDMI (Pro-specific)
faults.fix: Identify shorted cap/branch via diode mode or thermal cam. Remove shorted component. If short clears, replace. If persists, trace to IC.
faults.pro_specific_note: A2918 has additional PP3V8_AON branches for HDMI, SD card, and dual speaker amps that A2681 Air doesn't have. More potential short paths.

faults.name: PPBUS_AON short
root_causes: Liquid damage on main bus trace
Shorted component pulling bus to ground
faults.fix: Diode mode on PPBUS_AON. Find shorted cap. Remove and retest.

faults.name: PP3V3_S2_LDO short
root_causes: CD3217 internal LDO short pulling PP3V3_UPC down
faults.fix: Diode mode on PP3V3_S2_LDO line. If UPC LDO shorted → CD3217 internal fault.

faults.name: U8100 (MPMU) failure
root_causes: Liquid damage on MPMU BGA pads
Downstream short loading MPMU outputs → MPMU shuts down
faults.test_points: MPMU analog probe points on page 47 (PP57xx series)
faults.fix: If multiple LDO outputs missing → replace U8100. If single output missing → find downstream short.

faults.name: SSD / NAND power fault
faults.fix: Check NAND power rails. Check fuses FL4000/FL4001. If SSD power IC shorted → replace.

faults.name: PP5V_S2 (UC260 TPS62130) path fault
root_causes: UC260 (TPS62130) dead — different IC from A2681 (which used UC300)
faults.fix: Check UC260 input/output. Check enable signal. If both OK → UC260 dead.
faults.note: A2918 uses TPS62130 (UC260) not UC300 like A2681. Different pinout, different passives.

faults.name: Liquid damage — multiple component failure
faults.frequency: High (~40% of all fault records on similar boards)
faults.affected_rail: Multiple — depends on liquid ingress location
faults.fix: Clean logic board thoroughly (IPA + ultrasonic). Identify all shorts. Fix one at a time.
faults.pro_specific_note: A2918 has more exposed connector areas (HDMI, SD card) than Air models, providing additional liquid ingress paths.

faults.name: HDMI circuit fault (Pro-specific)
faults.frequency: Low (new failure mode for Pro models)

symptoms.dcps_20v: Normal current but no HDMI output, OR 0.1-0.5A if HDMI circuit shorted

faults.fix: Check HDMI power rails. If PP3V8_AON_HDMI shorted → isolate to find if Cobra or passive component. Check UH908 buck output.
faults.note: This fault type does not exist on MacBook Air models.

faults.name: SD card circuit fault (Pro-specific)

symptoms.dcps_20v: Normal current but SD card not detected

faults.fix: Check SD card power rails. Check UK550 for short. Check connector for physical damage.
faults.note: This fault type does not exist on MacBook Air models.

faults.name: MagSafe charging fault

symptoms.dcps_20v: Normal on USB-C but MagSafe doesn't charge

faults.fix: Check F5601 continuity. Check Q5401 MOSFET pair. If both OK → UPC5 fault.
